name: Segment tree with lazy propagation (range-add + range-sum)
task: Create /tmp/eval_segtree/seg.py with class SegTree. Constructor SegTree(arr) builds the tree from a list of integers (the underlying array uses 0-based half-open intervals). Methods: update(l, r, v) adds v to every element in arr[l:r] (l inclusive, r exclusive). query(l, r) returns the sum of arr[l:r]. Both update and query must be O(log n) using lazy propagation — do NOT re-walk the leaves on every update; defer pending updates with lazy tags and push them down only when needed.

validation.script:
import importlib.util, random; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_segtree/seg.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); random.seed(42); n = 200; arr = [random.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]; seg = mod.SegTree(list(arr)); ref = list(arr)
for _ in range(800):
    op = random.choice(['u','u','q','q','q'])
    l = random.randint(0, n-1); r = random.randint(l+1, n)
    if op == 'u':
        v = random.randint(-50, 50); seg.update(l, r, v)
        for i in range(l, r): ref[i] += v
    else:
        assert seg.query(l, r) == sum(ref[l:r]), f'mismatch on [{l},{r}): got {seg.query(l, r)} want {sum(ref[l:r])}'
import time
big = list(range(20000)); big_seg = mod.SegTree(big); start = time.time()
for _ in range(5000):
    big_seg.update(random.randint(0,19999), random.randint(0,20000), 1)
    big_seg.query(random.randint(0,19999), random.randint(0,20000))
elapsed = time.time() - start
assert elapsed < 2.5, f'too slow: {elapsed:.2f}s — likely missing lazy propagation'
print('OK')
